goal: Produce a concrete remediation plan for department import parsing and autocomplete behavior based on the latest review findings.
deliverable: A frozen requirement doc and execution plan covering parser hardening, autocomplete policy alignment, regression proof, and verification gates.
constraints: Do not introduce a hardcoded department list
Preserve the current import preview/manual correction path
Keep remediation bounded to department parsing, autocomplete semantics, and proof artifacts unless a directly related defect appears
Do not claim the issue is fixed before implementation and evidence
acceptance_criteria: Parser false-positive department extraction risk is addressed in the plan
Autocomplete source-of-truth policy is made explicit in the plan
Verification commands and manual spot checks are defined
Governed requirement and plan artifacts are written to the repo
product_acceptance_criteria: Imported department preview is correct on a representative happy-path document
Ambiguous department regions do not silently become handler/date text
Ledger filter department options match the approved policy
manual_spot_checks: Import a known-good sample and verify department preview correctness
Import an edge sample with blank or split department area and verify preview leaves department empty with warning
Verify ledger department options after creating and soft-deleting sample rows
completion_language_policy: Planning-only language until implementation, validation, regression, and spot checks all succeed.
delivery_truth_contract: This run freezes planning artifacts only; it does not claim code remediation is complete.
non_goals: Broad OCR/parser refactors outside department extraction
inferred_assumptions: Departments should continue to come from imported or created records rather than hardcoded defaults
Parser false positives are the highest-severity defect in the reviewed chain
Soft-delete autocomplete behavior still requires explicit product policy approval
